name: ulw-loop
tagline: Orchestrates goal-based, evidence-bound work loops that spawn/track Codex subagents and drive an external `omo ulw-loop` CLI, including auto
verdict: No secret access, network egress, obfuscation, or hidden instructions found. Two real risks: it directs the agent to autonomously commit code ('commit each verified work unit atomically') and to force-overwrite goal-state evidence via `--force`, both without a described per-action user confirmation step. Most substantive procedure also lives in an unreviewed

highlights.summary: Directs autonomous 'commit each verified work unit atomically' (line 34) and overwriting goal state with `--force` 'to intentionally overwrite completed evidence' (line 24) — both irreversible actions

Frontmatter description only claims 'decompose work into systematic, evidence-bound steps,' but the body also mandates autonomous git commits, multi-agent spawning, and state-file force-overwrite — br
No 'do not use when...' guidance anywhere in the file.
